perturbation.original_text: Unless a shorter period is indicated in the applicable CITR, the disclosing party will not insert any claims of breach of this Agreement or misappropriation of trade secrets against the receiving party arising from the receiving party's disclosure of the disclosing party's Confidential Information made more than five <PAGE> 2 (5) years from the date of the CITR under which such information was disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless a shorter period is indicated in the applicable CITR, the disclosing party will not insert any claims of breach of this Agreement or misappropriation of trade secrets against the receiving party arising from the receiving party's disclosure of the disclosing party's Confidential Information made more than one (1) year from the date of the CITR under which such information was disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: This modification shortens the period for claims from five years to one year. This directly contradicts the later clause specifying that the receiving party must continue to treat the information as confidential, undermining its enforceability due to the reduced claim period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: However, unless at least one of the exceptions set for in Section 4 below has occurred, the receiving party will continue to treat such confidential Information as the confidential information of the disclosing party and only disclose any such Confidential Information to third parties under the terms of a non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the claim period from five years to one year. The contradicted text requires the receiving party to treat the information as confidential even after the original claim period expires, essentially requiring indefinite confidentiality. By reducing the claim period, the enforceability of maintaining confidentiality is undermined, since the disclosing party has limited recourse if the receiving party breaches confidentiality after the one-year period. The change introduces conflicting obligations regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon written notice to the other party: provided that each party's obligations with respect to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement will survive any such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon written notice to the other party. Upon termination, all obligations with respect to Confidential Information are immediately null and void.
perturbation.explanation: This modification directly contradicts the original clause stating that obligations related to Confidential Information survive termination. Now, termination voids all such obligations, creating uncertainty and significantly weakening the protections afforded to the disclosing party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either party may, at any time: (a) cease giving Confidential Information to the other party without any liability and/or (b) request in writing the return or destruction of all or part of its Confidential Information previously disclosed, and all copies thereof, and the receiving party will promptly comply with such request, and certify in writing its compliance.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that upon termination, all obligations with respect to Confidential Information are immediately null and void. This directly contradicts the original agreement's clause, which is the contradicted text, which states the receiving party will return or destroy all confidential information. If the changed text makes obligations regarding confidential information void, that contradicts the requirement to return or destroy such information, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The explanation clarifies that obligations no longer survive termination.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, either party may use Residuals for any purpose, including without limitation use in development manufacture, promotion, sale and maintenance of its products or services: provided that this right to Residuals does not represent a licence under any patents, copyrights or other intellectual property rights of the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, neither party may use Residuals for any purpose, including without limitation use in development manufacture, promotion, sale and maintenance of its products or services: provided that this right to Residuals does not represent a licence under any patents, copyrights or other intellectual property rights of the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'either party may use Residuals' to 'neither party may use Residuals' creates a direct conflict. The contradiction arises because, elsewhere in the agreement, the free use of Residuals is implicitly assumed as part of the risk assessment for disclosing confidential information. Now, the prohibition affects how the information can be used and what the parties can do after the disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company understands that Intel is a diverse corporation which conducts research and development activities in an immense variety of technologies, often resulting in new commercial product development. The Company acknowledges that Intel may already be working on similar technology as that disclosed by the Company, and that Intel's personnel to whom the disclosure is made may be wholly unaware of this work. Intel's receipt of Confidential Information under this agreement shall not create any obligation in any way limiting, restricting, or prohibiting Intel's assignment of employees or contractors. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, Intel may use residuals of the Confidential Information for any purpose including without limitation use in development, manufacture, promotion, sale and maintenance of Intel's products and services.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'either party may use Residuals' to 'neither party may use Residuals' directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states 'Intel may use residuals of the Confidential Information for any purpose'. The original text and the contradicted text are consistent, but the change introduces a direct conflict, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.
